---
title: NY HP Rate Design Analysis

toc: true
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom

appendix-style: default
citation-location: document
citation:
  container-title: Switchbox

format:
  html:
    page-layout: full

execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook analyzes CAIRO simulation outputs for New York heat pump rate
design. It produces two families of visualization:

1. **Bill change histograms** — weighted distributions of annual total energy
   bill changes after switching to heat pumps, by population subgroup.
2. **Median electric bill component breakdown** — stacked bar chart decomposing
   the weighted-median electric bill into supply, delivery volumetric, and
   delivery fixed charges for current HVAC vs heat pump scenarios.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

import math

import yaml

import polars as pl
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    coord_cartesian,
    coord_flip,
    element_blank,
    element_text,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_col,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    guides,
    labs,
    position_stack,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_discrete,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

UTILITY = "coned"
BATCH = "ny_20260307_r1-8_gascalcfix"
STATE = "NY"
RDP_REF = "d0353e1"

S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_3+4/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_12 = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
```

## Constants

```{python}
#| label: constants

ANNUAL_MONTH = "Annual"
BLDG_ID = "bldg_id"
HEATING_TYPE_COL = "postprocess_group.heating_type"
```

## Data ingest

The master bills tables are pre-built, Hive-partitioned parquet datasets that
contain per-building monthly and annual bills with fully decomposed electric
components (fixed, delivery volumetric, supply), gas, oil, and propane — plus
ResStock metadata columns — across all NY utilities.

```{python}
#| label: ingest

bills_current = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34, hive_partitioning=True)
```

## Median energy bill components

Stacked bar chart of energy bill components (electric delivery fixed /
delivery volumetric / supply + gas) for the weighted-median natgas-heated
customer under current HVAC, and that same building after switching to a heat
pump. Both bars show the same building so the reader can see gas costs shrink
and electric costs grow for one household.

```{python}
#| label: bill-component-helpers

def bill_components_for_scenario(
    bills_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    utility: str | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Extract annual bill components (electric + gas + delivered fuels) from master bills."""
    filtered = bills_lf.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    if utility is not None:
        filtered = filtered.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == utility)
    return (
        filtered
        .select(
            BLDG_ID, "weight",
            pl.col("elec_supply_bill").alias("supply"),
            pl.col("elec_delivery_bill").alias("delivery_volumetric"),
            pl.col("elec_fixed_charge").alias("delivery_fixed"),
            pl.col("gas_total_bill").alias("gas"),
            pl.col("propane_total_bill").alias("propane"),
            pl.col("oil_total_bill").alias("oil"),
            pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("energy_total"),
            "heats_with_natgas",
        )
        .collect()
    )


def weighted_median_row(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    sort_col: str = "energy_total",
    weight_col: str = "weight",
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Return the single row at the weighted median by sort_col."""
    total_weight = df[weight_col].sum()
    sorted_df = df.sort(sort_col)
    cum = sorted_df[weight_col].cum_sum()
    idx = (cum >= 0.5 * total_weight).arg_max()  # first True in a bool Series = first row past 50%
    assert idx is not None, "Empty data or non-positive weights"
    return sorted_df.slice(idx, 1)


SELECT_COLS = [
    BLDG_ID, "weight",
    "supply", "delivery_volumetric", "delivery_fixed", "gas",
    "propane", "oil", "energy_total",
]
```

```{python}
#| label: bill-component-plot-helpers

SCENARIO_ORDER = ["Current HVAC", "Air-source heat pump\n(mid-efficiency)"]

COMPONENT_NAMES: dict[str, str] = {
    "delivery_fixed": "Delivery Charge (Fixed)",
    "delivery_volumetric": "Delivery Charge (Volumetric)",
    "supply": "Supply Charge",
    "gas": "Gas Bill",
}
COMPONENT_ORDER = list(COMPONENT_NAMES.values())
SIMPLE_COMPONENT_ORDER = ["Electric Bill", "Gas Bill"]

SIMPLE_COLORS = {
    "Electric Bill": "#FFC729",
    "Gas Bill": "#C8D66A",
}
SIMPLE_LABEL_COLORS = {**SIMPLE_COLORS, "Gas Bill": "#7A8A10"}

DECOMPOSED_COLORS = {
    "Delivery Charge (Fixed)": "#023047",
    "Delivery Charge (Volumetric)": "#56B4E9",
    "Supply Charge": "#E69F00",
    "Gas Bill": "#CCCCCC",
}
DECOMPOSED_LABEL_COLORS = {**DECOMPOSED_COLORS, "Gas Bill": "#888888"}

LINE_X = 2.35
LABEL_X = 2.45


def _format_dollar(v: float) -> str:
    return f"${v:,.0f}"


def _dollar_labels(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return df.with_columns(
        pl.col("value")
        .map_elements(_format_dollar, return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("label"),
    )


def _totals_df(plot_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return _dollar_labels(
        plot_df.group_by("scenario").agg(pl.col("value").sum())
    )


def _side_label_annotations(
    segments: list[tuple[str, float, float]],
    label_colors: dict[str, str],
) -> list:
    """Build annotate() layers for side-labels pointing at bar 2 segments.

    *segments* is a list of ``(label_text, bottom_y, height)`` tuples.
    """
    annotations: list = []
    for label_text, bottom, height in segments:
        y_mid = bottom + height / 2
        color_key = label_text.replace("\n", " ")
        color = label_colors[color_key]
        annotations.append(annotate(
            "segment",
            x=LINE_X, xend=LABEL_X - 0.03,
            y=y_mid, yend=y_mid,
            color=color, size=0.5,
        ))
        annotations.append(annotate(
            "text",
            x=LABEL_X, y=y_mid,
            label=label_text,
            color=color, ha="left", va="center",
            size=8, fontweight="bold",
        ))
    return annotations


# ── Simple chart (gas + electric, no decomposition) ──────────────────────────

def unpivot_median_simple(median_df: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Aggregate electric sub-components into one 'Electric Bill' row."""
    electric = float(
        median_df["supply"][0]
        + median_df["delivery_volumetric"][0]
        + median_df["delivery_fixed"][0]
    )
    gas = float(median_df["gas"][0])
    return pl.DataFrame({
        "component": ["Electric Bill", "Gas Bill"],
        "value": [electric, gas],
        "scenario": [scenario, scenario],
    })


def plot_bill_components_simple(
    med_current: pl.DataFrame,
    med_hp: pl.DataFrame,
    title: str = "Median energy bill components",
) -> ggplot:
    """Stacked bar: total electric vs gas for current HVAC and heat pump."""
    long = pl.concat([
        unpivot_median_simple(med_current, SCENARIO_ORDER[0]),
        unpivot_median_simple(med_hp, SCENARIO_ORDER[1]),
    ])

    scenario_dtype = pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)
    component_dtype = pl.Enum(SIMPLE_COMPONENT_ORDER)

    plot_df = _dollar_labels(long.with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(scenario_dtype),
        pl.col("component").cast(component_dtype),
    ))
    totals = _totals_df(plot_df)

    electric_hp = float(
        med_hp["supply"][0] + med_hp["delivery_volumetric"][0] + med_hp["delivery_fixed"][0]
    )
    gas_hp = float(med_hp["gas"][0])
    side_labels = _side_label_annotations(
        [("Electric Bill", 0, electric_hp), ("Gas Bill", electric_hp, gas_hp)],
        SIMPLE_LABEL_COLORS,
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="component"))
        + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
        + geom_text(
            aes(label="label"),
            position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
            color="white", fontweight="bold", size=11,
        )
        + geom_text(
            aes(x="scenario", y="value", label="label"),
            data=totals,
            va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
            size=11, nudge_y=30, inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=SIMPLE_COLORS)
        + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER, expand=(0, 0.6, 0, 1.8))
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="Annual energy bill ($)", title=title)
        + theme_minimal()
    )
    for sl in side_labels:
        p = p + sl
    return p


# ── Decomposed chart (electric sub-components + muted gas + delivery line) ───

def unpivot_median(median_df: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Unpivot a single-row median into long-form (scenario, component, value)."""
    cols = list(COMPONENT_NAMES.keys())
    return (
        median_df.select(cols)
        .unpivot(on=cols, variable_name="col", value_name="value")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("col").replace_strict(COMPONENT_NAMES).alias("component"),
            pl.lit(scenario).alias("scenario"),
        )
        .drop("col")
    )


def plot_bill_components_decomposed(
    med_current: pl.DataFrame,
    med_hp: pl.DataFrame,
    title: str = "Median energy bill components",
) -> ggplot:
    """Stacked bar with electric decomposition, muted gas, and delivery reference line."""
    long = pl.concat([
        unpivot_median(med_current, SCENARIO_ORDER[0]),
        unpivot_median(med_hp, SCENARIO_ORDER[1]),
    ])

    scenario_dtype = pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)
    component_dtype = pl.Enum(COMPONENT_ORDER)

    plot_df = _dollar_labels(long.with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(scenario_dtype),
        pl.col("component").cast(component_dtype),
    ))
    totals = _totals_df(plot_df)

    delivery_top = float(
        med_current["delivery_fixed"][0] + med_current["delivery_volumetric"][0]
    )

    fixed_hp = float(med_hp["delivery_fixed"][0])
    vol_hp = float(med_hp["delivery_volumetric"][0])
    supply_hp = float(med_hp["supply"][0])
    gas_hp = float(med_hp["gas"][0])
    side_labels = _side_label_annotations(
        [
            ("Delivery Charge\n(Fixed)", 0, fixed_hp),
            ("Delivery Charge\n(Volumetric)", fixed_hp, vol_hp),
            ("Supply Charge", fixed_hp + vol_hp, supply_hp),
            ("Gas Bill", fixed_hp + vol_hp + supply_hp, gas_hp),
        ],
        DECOMPOSED_LABEL_COLORS,
    )

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="component"))
        + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
        + geom_text(
            aes(label="label"),
            position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
            color="white", fontweight="bold", size=11,
        )
        + geom_text(
            aes(x="scenario", y="value", label="label"),
            data=totals,
            va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
            size=11, nudge_y=30, inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + annotate(
            "segment",
            x=0.7, xend=2.3,
            y=delivery_top, yend=delivery_top,
            linetype="dashed", color="#FFC729", size=1.2,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=DECOMPOSED_COLORS)
        + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER, expand=(0, 0.6, 0, 1.8))
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="Annual energy bill ($)", title=title)
        + theme_minimal()
    )
    for sl in side_labels:
        p = p + sl
    return p
```

### Utility-level

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-components

natgas_current = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_current, UTILITY)
    .filter(                                                                     # <1>
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas")                                              # <1>
        & (pl.col("propane") == 0)                                               # <1>
        & (pl.col("oil") == 0)                                                   # <1>
    )                                                                            # <1>
)
assert not natgas_current.is_empty(), "No natgas-only buildings in current-HVAC scenario"

hp_same_bldgs = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_hp, UTILITY)
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(natgas_current[BLDG_ID].to_list()))           # <2>
)
assert not hp_same_bldgs.is_empty(), "No matching buildings in HP scenario"

median_current_utility = weighted_median_row(natgas_current.select(SELECT_COLS))
median_bldg_id = median_current_utility[BLDG_ID][0]
median_hp_utility = (                                                            # <3>
    hp_same_bldgs                                                                # <3>
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id)                                  # <3>
    .select(SELECT_COLS)                                                         # <3>
)                                                                                # <3>
assert len(median_hp_utility) == 1, f"Expected 1 row for bldg {median_bldg_id} in HP, got {len(median_hp_utility)}"
```

1. Keep only natgas-heated buildings with zero propane and zero oil bills (~3.3% of ConEd natgas buildings are dropped). This ensures the visible bar components (electric + gas) equal the full energy total with nothing hidden.
2. Pre-filter the HP scenario to the same natgas-only building IDs so we can look up individual buildings.
3. Reuse the same building (not a new median) for the HP bar. Two different buildings at the median would have different gas baselines, making it impossible to see gas costs shrink after electrification. Showing the same household keeps the comparison apples-to-apples.

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-simple
#| fig-cap: "Median energy bill components — ConEd"

plot_bill_components_simple(median_current_utility, median_hp_utility, "Median energy bill components — ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-decomposed
#| fig-cap: "Median energy bill components (decomposed) — ConEd"

plot_bill_components_decomposed(median_current_utility, median_hp_utility, "Median energy bill components — ConEd")
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-components-state

natgas_current_state = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_current)
    .filter(                                                                     # <1>
        pl.col("heats_with_natgas")                                              # <1>
        & (pl.col("propane") == 0)                                               # <1>
        & (pl.col("oil") == 0)                                                   # <1>
    )                                                                            # <1>
)
assert not natgas_current_state.is_empty(), "No natgas-only buildings statewide (current)"

hp_same_bldgs_state = (
    bill_components_for_scenario(bills_hp)
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(natgas_current_state[BLDG_ID].to_list()))     # <2>
)
assert not hp_same_bldgs_state.is_empty(), "No matching buildings statewide (HP)"

median_current_state = weighted_median_row(natgas_current_state.select(SELECT_COLS))
median_bldg_id_state = median_current_state[BLDG_ID][0]
median_hp_state = (                                                              # <3>
    hp_same_bldgs_state                                                          # <3>
    .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID) == median_bldg_id_state)                             # <3>
    .select(SELECT_COLS)                                                         # <3>
)                                                                                # <3>
assert len(median_hp_state) == 1, f"Expected 1 row for bldg {median_bldg_id_state} in HP, got {len(median_hp_state)}"
```

1. Same natgas-only filter as the ConEd cell. Statewide, ~2.7% of natgas buildings are dropped.
2. Pre-filter the HP scenario to the same natgas-only building IDs so we can look up individual buildings.
3. Same-building lookup (see ConEd annotation above).

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-simple-state
#| fig-cap: "Median energy bill components — Statewide"

plot_bill_components_simple(median_current_state, median_hp_state, "Median energy bill components — Statewide")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-decomposed-state
#| fig-cap: "Median energy bill components (decomposed) — Statewide"

plot_bill_components_decomposed(median_current_state, median_hp_state, "Median energy bill components — Statewide")
```

## Bill change distributions

Weighted histograms of annual total energy bill change (before vs after switching
to heat pumps) under default rates. The master bills `energy_total_bill` already
includes electric, gas, oil, and propane costs.

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-deltas

DELTA_SELECT = [
    BLDG_ID, "weight",
    pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_before"),
    "postprocess_group.has_hp",
    HEATING_TYPE_COL,
    "heats_with_natgas",
    "heats_with_oil",
    "heats_with_propane",
]

annual_current = (
    bills_current
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_hp = (
    bills_hp
    .filter((pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH) & (pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY))
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_with_meta = annual_current.join(annual_hp, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)

annual_current_state = (
    bills_current
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(DELTA_SELECT)
    .collect()
)
annual_hp_state = (
    bills_hp
    .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(BLDG_ID, pl.col("energy_total_bill").alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)
delta_state = annual_current_state.join(annual_hp_state, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)
```

```{python}
#| label: histogram-helpers

BIN_WIDTH = 100
QUADRANT_COLORS = {
    "savings > $1k": "#1b5e20",
    "savings $0-1k": "#81c784",
    "losses $0-1k": "#ef9a9a",
    "losses > $1k": "#b71c1c",
}
QUADRANT_ORDER = list(QUADRANT_COLORS.keys())
QUADRANT_LABELS = {
    "losses > $1k": "LOSE > $1K",
    "losses $0-1k": "LOSE $0-1K",
    "savings $0-1k": "SAVE $0-1K",
    "savings > $1k": "SAVE > $1K",
}


def _weighted_percentile(df: pl.DataFrame, col: str, w_col: str, q: float) -> float:
    """Weighted percentile via cumulative weight."""
    s = df.sort(col)
    cum = s[w_col].cum_sum() / s[w_col].sum()
    return float(s.filter(cum >= q)[col][0])


def quadrant_pcts(df: pl.DataFrame) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Weighted percentage of homes in each bill-change quadrant."""
    total = df["weight"].sum()
    return {
        "savings > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "savings $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= -1000) & (pl.col("delta") < 0))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses $0-1k": df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= 0) & (pl.col("delta") < 1000))["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
        "losses > $1k": df.filter(pl.col("delta") >= 1000)["weight"].sum() / total * 100,
    }


def plot_bill_change_histogram(df: pl.DataFrame) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of annual bill change."""
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, "delta", "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            (
                (pl.col("delta") / BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BIN_WIDTH + BIN_WIDTH / 2
            ).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000)
            .then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0))
            .then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000))
            .then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    pct = quadrant_pcts(df)

    quadrant_dtype = pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("quadrant").cast(quadrant_dtype))
    max_weight = (
        cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    )
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.05

    quadrant_bounds = [
        (x_lo, -1000),
        (-1000, 0),
        (0, 1000),
        (1000, x_hi),
    ]
    label_xs = [
        (max(lo, x_lo) + min(hi, x_hi)) / 2 for lo, hi in quadrant_bounds
    ]

    annotations = []
    for label, x_pos in zip(QUADRANT_ORDER, label_xs):
        if pct[label] < 0.05:
            continue
        annotations.append(
            annotate(
                "text", x=x_pos, y=y_annot,
                label=f"{label}\n{pct[label]:.1f}%",
                ha="center", size=11, color=QUADRANT_COLORS[label],
            )
        )

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(width=BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + labs(
            x="Annual Bill Change ($)",
            y="# of Homes",
            title="Change in Total Energy Bills after Switching to Heat Pumps",
        )
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(figure_size=(14, 6))
    )
    for a in annotations:
        p = p + a
    return p


def plot_quadrant_bar(
    rows: list[tuple[str, pl.DataFrame]],
    title: str = "How bills would change after switching to heat pumps:",
) -> ggplot:
    """Horizontal stacked bar(s) showing quadrant proportions.

    *rows* is a list of ``(label, df)`` pairs where each *df* has ``delta``
    and ``weight`` columns. Each pair becomes one bar. Pass a single pair for
    one bar; pass multiple pairs for side-by-side comparison (e.g. different
    rate scenarios).
    """
    records: list[dict] = []
    all_pcts: list[dict[str, float]] = []
    for row_label, df in rows:
        pct = quadrant_pcts(df)
        all_pcts.append(pct)
        for q in QUADRANT_ORDER:
            records.append({"scenario": row_label, "quadrant": q, "pct": pct[q]})

    scenario_order = [r[0] for r in reversed(rows)]
    plot_df = pl.DataFrame(records).with_columns(
        pl.col("quadrant").cast(pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)),
        pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(scenario_order)),
    )

    n_scenarios = len(rows)
    first_pct = all_pcts[0]
    stacking_order = list(reversed(QUADRANT_ORDER))
    extra_annotations: list = []

    cum = 0.0
    for q in stacking_order:
        mid = cum + first_pct[q] / 2
        cum += first_pct[q]
        if first_pct[q] < 2:
            continue
        extra_annotations.append(
            annotate(
                "text",
                x=n_scenarios + 0.45,
                y=mid,
                label=QUADRANT_LABELS[q],
                ha="center", va="bottom",
                size=8, color=QUADRANT_COLORS[q], fontweight="bold",
            )
        )

    for i, (row_label, _) in enumerate(rows):
        bar_pos = n_scenarios - i
        extra_annotations.append(
            annotate(
                "text",
                x=bar_pos,
                y=101,
                label=row_label,
                ha="left", va="center",
                size=9, fontweight="bold",
            )
        )

    top_expand = 0.55 if n_scenarios > 1 else 1.2

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="pct", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(position="stack", width=0.55)
        + geom_text(
            plot_df.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3),
            aes(label="pct"),
            position=position_stack(vjust=0.5),
            format_string="{:.0f}%",
            color="white",
            size=10,
            fontweight="bold",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
        + scale_x_discrete(expand=(0, 0.35, 0, top_expand))
        + coord_flip()
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="", title=title)
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(
            figure_size=(14, max(2.2, 0.8 + 1.0 * n_scenarios)),
            axis_text_y=element_blank(),
            axis_text_x=element_blank(),
            plot_title=element_text(size=11, ha="left", margin={"b": -8, "units": "pt"}),
            panel_grid=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_major=element_blank(),
        )
    )
    for a in extra_annotations:
        p = p + a
    return p
```

### ConEd

#### Non-HP households

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — non-heat-pump households"

non_hp = (
    delta_with_meta
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(non_hp)
```

#### Natural gas heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — natural gas-heated households"

natgas = (
    delta_with_meta
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas)
```

#### Oil / propane heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — oil/propane-heated households"

oil_propane = (
    delta_with_meta
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane)
```

#### Electric resistance heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — electric resistance-heated households"

elec_resistance = (
    delta_with_meta
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance)
```

#### Quadrant summary bars

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-non-hp
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — non-heat-pump households"

plot_quadrant_bar([("Non-HP households", non_hp)])
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-natgas
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — natural gas-heated households"

plot_quadrant_bar([("Natural gas", natgas)])
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-oil-propane
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — oil/propane-heated households"

plot_quadrant_bar([("Oil / propane", oil_propane)])
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-elec-resistance
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary — electric resistance-heated households"

plot_quadrant_bar([("Electric resistance", elec_resistance)])
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-all
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary by heating fuel — default rates"

plot_quadrant_bar([
    ("All non-HP", non_hp),
    ("Natural gas", natgas),
    ("Oil / propane", oil_propane),
    ("Electric resistance", elec_resistance),
])
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — non-heat-pump households (Statewide)"

non_hp_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False)
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(non_hp_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — natural gas-heated households (Statewide)"

natgas_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(natgas_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — oil/propane-heated households (Statewide)"

oil_propane_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(oil_propane_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance-state
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — electric resistance-heated households (Statewide)"

elec_resistance_state = (
    delta_state
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .select("delta", "weight")
)
plot_bill_change_histogram(elec_resistance_state)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant-bar-all-state
#| fig-cap: "Bill change summary by heating fuel — Statewide"

plot_quadrant_bar([
    ("All non-HP", non_hp_state),
    ("Natural gas", natgas_state),
    ("Oil / propane", oil_propane_state),
    ("Electric resistance", elec_resistance_state),
])
```


## Bill Alignment Test (BAT) results

The master BAT table contains per-building annual bill alignment values
decomposed into delivery, supply, and total components. A positive
`BAT_percustomer` value means the customer's bill exceeds their marginal cost
of service — they cross-subsidize other customers. A negative value means they
pay less than their cost — others cross-subsidize them.

We look at all customers under current rates (upgrade 0 / run 1+2) to quantify
how current rate structures misalign bills with cost of service.
`BAT_percustomer_total` is the sum of delivery and supply components. The
unified histograms show the full population; the faceted versions break the
distribution down by heating type (fossil fuel, electric resistance, heat pump).

```{python}
#| label: bat-ingest

bat_12 = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_12, hive_partitioning=True)

bat_all = (
    bat_12
    .select(
        BLDG_ID, "weight",
        "BAT_percustomer_total",
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "sb.electric_utility",
    )
    .collect()
)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-histogram-helpers

from plotnine import facet_wrap

BAT_BIN_WIDTH = 50
BAT_COLORS = {"overpaying": "#b71c1c", "underpaying": "#1b5e20"}


def plot_bat_histogram(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total): HP households under current rates",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer values."""
    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2)
            .alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0)
            .then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    total_weight = df["weight"].sum()
    pct_over = df.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / total_weight * 100
    pct_under = 100 - pct_over
    w_median = _weighted_percentile(df, col, "weight", 0.5)

    max_weight = cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.08

    p = (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=w_median, linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7)
        + annotate(
            "text", x=w_median, y=y_annot,
            label=f"median: ${w_median:,.0f}",
            ha="left" if w_median >= 0 else "right",
            size=9, color="#333333",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(figure_size=(14, 6))
    )

    if pct_under >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(x_lo + min(0, x_hi)) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Underpaying\n{pct_under:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#1b5e20",
        )
    if pct_over >= 1:
        p = p + annotate(
            "text", x=(max(0, x_lo) + x_hi) / 2, y=y_annot,
            label=f"Overpaying\n{pct_over:.0f}%",
            ha="center", size=10, color="#b71c1c",
        )

    return p


HEATING_TYPE_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "fossil_fuel": "Fossil fuel",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
}
FACET_ORDER = ["Electric resistance", "Heat pump", "Fossil fuel", "Other"]


def plot_bat_histogram_faceted(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    col: str = "BAT_percustomer_total",
    title: str = "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type",
) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of BAT per-customer, vertically faceted by heating type."""
    facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)
    labeled = df.with_columns(
        pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
        .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
        .fill_null("Other")
        .cast(facet_dtype)
        .alias("heating_label"),
    )

    x_lo = math.floor(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.01) / 500) * 500
    x_hi = math.ceil(_weighted_percentile(labeled, col, "weight", 0.99) / 500) * 500

    binned = (
        labeled.with_columns(
            ((pl.col(col) / BAT_BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BAT_BIN_WIDTH + BAT_BIN_WIDTH / 2)
            .alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") < 0)
            .then(pl.lit("underpaying"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("overpaying"))
            .alias("sign"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "sign", "heating_label")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    sign_dtype = pl.Enum(["underpaying", "overpaying"])
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("sign").cast(sign_dtype))

    stat_rows = []
    median_rows = []
    for ht in [f for f in FACET_ORDER if f in labeled["heating_label"].unique().to_list()]:
        sub = labeled.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        tw = sub["weight"].sum()
        pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / tw * 100
        w_med = _weighted_percentile(sub, col, "weight", 0.5)
        facet_bins = plot_df.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
        y_max = cast(float, facet_bins["weight_sum"].max()) if not facet_bins.is_empty() else 0.0
        stat_rows.append({
            "heating_label": ht,
            "x": x_hi,
            "y": y_max * 0.92,
            "label": f"median: ${w_med:,.0f}  ·  {pct_over:.0f}% overpaying",
        })
        median_rows.append({"heating_label": ht, "med_x": w_med})

    stats_df = pl.DataFrame(stat_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    medians_df = pl.DataFrame(median_rows).with_columns(pl.col("heating_label").cast(facet_dtype))
    n_facets = labeled["heating_label"].n_unique()

    return (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="sign"))
        + geom_col(width=BAT_BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="solid", color="gray", size=0.8)
        + geom_vline(
            data=medians_df, mapping=aes(xintercept="med_x"),
            linetype="dashed", color="#333333", size=0.7,
        )
        + geom_text(
            stats_df, aes(x="x", y="y", label="label"),
            ha="right", size=8, color="#333333",
            inherit_aes=False,
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BAT_COLORS)
        + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.15, 0))
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(x_lo, x_hi))
        + facet_wrap("heating_label", ncol=1, scales="free_y")
        + labs(x="BAT per-customer ($/year)", y="# of Homes (weighted)", title=title)
        + guides(fill=False)
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(figure_size=(14, 3 + 3 * n_facets))
    )
```

### ConEd

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, ConEd"

bat_coned = bat_all.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == UTILITY)
plot_bat_histogram(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) — ConEd")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-coned-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_coned, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — ConEd")
```

### Statewide

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) — all households, Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram(bat_all, title="BAT per-customer (total) — Statewide")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bat-histogram-state-faceted
#| fig-cap: "BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide"

plot_bat_histogram_faceted(bat_all, title="BAT per-customer (total) by heating type — Statewide")
```

### Summary statistics

```{python}
#| label: bat-summary-stats

col = "BAT_percustomer_total"
facet_dtype = pl.Enum(FACET_ORDER)

labeled_all = bat_all.with_columns(
    pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL)
    .replace_strict(HEATING_TYPE_LABELS, default=pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL))
    .fill_null("Other")
    .cast(facet_dtype)
    .alias("heating_label"),
)

total_weight = labeled_all["weight"].sum()

summary_rows = []
for ht in FACET_ORDER:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    w = sub["weight"].sum()
    median_bat = _weighted_percentile(sub, col, "weight", 0.5)
    pct_over = sub.filter(pl.col(col) >= 0)["weight"].sum() / w * 100
    summary_rows.append({
        "Heating type": ht,
        "Weighted median BAT ($/yr)": f"${median_bat:,.0f}",
        "% overpaying": f"{pct_over:.0f}%",
        "% of households": f"{w / total_weight * 100:.1f}%",
    })

pl.DataFrame(summary_rows)
```

```{python}
#| label: bat-dollar-totals

dollar_rows = []
for ht in FACET_ORDER:
    sub = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == ht)
    if sub.is_empty():
        continue
    weighted_bat = sub[col] * sub["weight"]
    total_over = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat >= 0).sum()
    total_under = weighted_bat.filter(weighted_bat < 0).sum()
    dollar_rows.append({
        "Heating type": ht,
        "Total overpayment": f"${total_over:,.0f}",
        "Total underpayment": f"${total_under:,.0f}",
        "Net": f"${total_over + total_under:,.0f}",
    })

pl.DataFrame(dollar_rows)
```

### Impact of tech-specific rates on fossil fuel cross-subsidization

What happens to fossil fuel customers' cross-subsidization if tech-specific rates
zero out the net overpayment of HP customers, ER customers, or both? We spread the
released overpayment uniformly across fossil fuel households and report the shift
in their net, median annual BAT, and median monthly BAT (over a 6-month heating
season).

```{python}
#| label: bat-scenario-table

HEATING_MONTHS = 6

ff = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Fossil fuel")
ff_weight = ff["weight"].sum()
ff_median_before = _weighted_percentile(ff, col, "weight", 0.5)
ff_net_before = (ff[col] * ff["weight"]).sum()

er = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Electric resistance")
er_net = (er[col] * er["weight"]).sum()

hp = labeled_all.filter(pl.col("heating_label") == "Heat pump")
hp_net = (hp[col] * hp["weight"]).sum()

scenarios = [
    ("HP rate only", hp_net),
    ("ER rate only", er_net),
    ("HP + ER rates", hp_net + er_net),
]

scenario_rows = []
for name, released in scenarios:
    shift = released / ff_weight
    new_net = ff_net_before + released
    new_median = ff_median_before + shift
    scenario_rows.append({
        "Scenario": name,
        "Cross-subsidy removed": f"${released / 1e6:,.0f}M",
        "FF net after": f"${new_net / 1e6:,.0f}M",
        "FF median BAT before": f"${ff_median_before:,.0f}/yr",
        "FF median BAT after": f"${new_median:,.0f}/yr",
        "Monthly (heating)": f"${shift / HEATING_MONTHS:,.0f}/mo",
    })

pl.DataFrame(scenario_rows)
```


## Revenue requirement composition

Each utility's total residential revenue requirement — the amount it must
recover from residential ratepayers — breaks into three pieces:

1. **Delivery (Rate Case)** — the base T&D revenue requirement set in the
   utility's most recent rate case.
2. **Delivery Top-Ups** — additional delivery-side charges for policy programs
   (SBC, CES delivery, EV make-ready, DLM), DER credit recovery (VDER),
   and sunk-cost recovery (COVID arrears, late-payment waivers).
3. **Supply** — energy commodity, merchant function charge, and CES supply
   surcharge.

Data comes from the computed revenue requirement YAMLs in
`rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/rev_requirement/`.

```{python}
#| label: rev-req-data

RR_CONFIG_DIR = "rate_design/hp_rates/ny/config/rev_requirement"
UTILITY_CONFIGS: dict[str, str] = {
    "CenHud": "cenhud.yaml",
    "ConEd": "coned.yaml",
    "NiMo": "nimo.yaml",
    "NYSEG": "nyseg.yaml",
    "RG&E": "rge.yaml",
    "O&R": "or.yaml",
    "PSEG-LI": "psegli.yaml",
}


def load_rev_requirements(
    configs: dict[str, str],
    ref: str,
    config_dir: str = RR_CONFIG_DIR,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Fetch utility revenue requirement YAMLs from RDP and build a DataFrame."""
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for display_name, filename in configs.items():
        content = fetch_rdp_file(f"{config_dir}/{filename}", ref=ref)
        cfg = yaml.safe_load(content)
        rows.append({
            "utility": display_name,
            "delivery_rr": cfg["delivery_revenue_requirement_from_rate_case"],
            "delivery_topups": cfg["delivery_revenue_requirement_topups"],
            "supply_rr": cfg["supply_revenue_requirement_topups"],
        })
    return pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(
        (pl.col("delivery_rr") + pl.col("delivery_topups") + pl.col("supply_rr"))
        .alias("total"),
    )


rr_data = load_rev_requirements(UTILITY_CONFIGS, ref=RDP_REF)

utility_order = rr_data.sort("total", descending=True)["utility"].to_list()

RR_COMPONENTS: dict[str, str] = {
    "delivery_rr": "Delivery (Rate Case)",
    "delivery_topups": "Delivery Top-Ups",
    "supply_rr": "Supply",
}
RR_COMPONENT_ORDER = list(RR_COMPONENTS.values())
RR_COLORS = {
    "Delivery (Rate Case)": "#56B4E9",
    "Delivery Top-Ups": "#E69F00",
    "Supply": "#999999",
}

rr_long = (
    rr_data.unpivot(
        on=["delivery_rr", "delivery_topups", "supply_rr"],
        index=["utility", "total"],
        variable_name="component_raw",
        value_name="dollars",
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("component_raw").replace_strict(RR_COMPONENTS).alias("component"),
        (pl.col("dollars") / pl.col("total") * 100).alias("pct"),
        (pl.col("dollars") / 1e9).alias("dollars_b"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
        pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)),
    )
)

rr_totals = rr_data.with_columns(
    pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e9).alias("total_b"),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e9)
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.1f}B", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("total_label"),
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rev-req-absolute
#| fig-cap: "Residential revenue requirement by utility ($B)"

(
    ggplot(rr_long, aes(x="utility", y="dollars_b", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.65)
    + geom_text(
        aes(x="utility", y="total_b", label="total_label"),
        data=rr_totals,
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=9, nudge_y=0.05, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=RR_COLORS, breaks=RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
    + labs(x="", y="Billions ($)", title="Residential revenue requirement by utility", fill="")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rev-req-pct
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement composition (% of total)"

pct_labels = rr_long.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3).with_columns(
    pl.col("pct")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"{v:.0f}%", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("pct_label"),
)

(
    ggplot(rr_long, aes(x="utility", y="pct", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.65)
    + geom_text(
        pct_labels,
        aes(label="pct_label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=9,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=RR_COLORS, breaks=RR_COMPONENT_ORDER)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.02, 0))
    + labs(
        x="", y="% of total revenue requirement",
        title="Revenue requirement composition by utility",
        fill="",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
)
```

```{python}
#| label: rev-req-table

rr_table = rr_data.select(
    "utility",
    (pl.col("delivery_rr") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Delivery RR ($M)"),
    (pl.col("delivery_topups") / 1e6).round(1).alias("Delivery Top-Ups ($M)"),
    (pl.col("supply_rr") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Supply RR ($M)"),
    (pl.col("total") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Total ($M)"),
    (pl.col("delivery_topups") / pl.col("total") * 100).round(1).alias("Top-Ups % of Total"),
).sort("Total ($M)", descending=True)

rr_table
```

### Revenue requirement vs. marginal cost allocation

The BAT decomposes each customer's cost of service into **marginal cost**
(economic burden: Σ load × MC) and **residual cost** (revenue requirement minus
total marginal costs, allocated per customer). Aggregating across all customers
in a utility gives the utility-level split. This chart compares the revenue
requirement composition (from rate-case configs) against the MC / residual split
(from the BAT).

```{python}
#| label: bat-mc-residual-data

UTILITY_CODE_TO_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
    v.replace(".yaml", ""): k for k, v in UTILITY_CONFIGS.items()
}

bat_enriched = (
    pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BAT_12, hive_partitioning=True)
    .select(
        "sb.electric_utility", "weight",
        "economic_burden_total", "residual_share_total", "annual_bill_total",
    )
    .collect()
)

mc_residual = (
    bat_enriched
    .group_by("sb.electric_utility")
    .agg(
        (pl.col("economic_burden_total") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("total_mc"),
        (pl.col("residual_share_total") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("total_residual"),
        (pl.col("annual_bill_total") * pl.col("weight")).sum().alias("total_bill"),
    )
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("sb.electric_utility")
        .replace_strict(UTILITY_CODE_TO_DISPLAY)
        .alias("utility"),
    )
    .drop("sb.electric_utility")
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rr-vs-mc-residual
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement vs. marginal / residual cost allocation by utility"

MC_COMPONENTS: dict[str, str] = {
    "total_mc": "Marginal Cost",
    "total_residual": "Residual Cost",
}
MC_COMPONENT_ORDER = list(MC_COMPONENTS.values())
MC_COLORS = {
    "Marginal Cost": "#2ca02c",
    "Residual Cost": "#d62728",
}

BAR_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "rr": "Revenue\nRequirement",
    "mc": "Cost\nAllocation",
}
BAR_ORDER = list(BAR_LABELS.values())

rr_for_join = rr_data.select(
    "utility",
    (pl.col("delivery_rr") + pl.col("delivery_topups")).alias("delivery_total"),
    pl.col("supply_rr"),
    pl.col("total").alias("rr_total"),
)

combined = rr_for_join.join(mc_residual, on="utility")

records: list[dict] = []
for row in combined.iter_rows(named=True):
    u = row["utility"]
    rr_tot = row["rr_total"]
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["rr"], "component": "Delivery (Rate Case)", "dollars": row["delivery_total"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["rr"], "component": "Supply", "dollars": row["supply_rr"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "Marginal Cost", "dollars": row["total_mc"]})
    records.append({"utility": u, "bar": BAR_LABELS["mc"], "component": "Residual Cost", "dollars": row["total_residual"]})

ALL_COMPONENTS = ["Delivery (Rate Case)", "Supply", "Marginal Cost", "Residual Cost"]
ALL_COLORS = {**RR_COLORS, **MC_COLORS}

plot_df = (
    pl.DataFrame(records)
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
        pl.col("bar").cast(pl.Enum(BAR_ORDER)),
        pl.col("component").cast(pl.Enum(ALL_COMPONENTS)),
        (pl.col("dollars") / 1e9).alias("dollars_b"),
    )
)

(
    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bar", y="dollars_b", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.7)
    + scale_fill_manual(values=ALL_COLORS, breaks=ALL_COMPONENTS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.08, 0))
    + facet_wrap("utility", ncol=4, scales="free_y")
    + labs(x="", y="Billions ($)", title="Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation", fill="")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(16, 8),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-rr-vs-mc-residual-pct
#| fig-cap: "Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation (% of total)"

bar_totals = (
    plot_df.group_by("utility", "bar")
    .agg(pl.col("dollars").sum().alias("bar_total"))
)
plot_df_pct = (
    plot_df.join(bar_totals, on=["utility", "bar"])
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("dollars") / pl.col("bar_total") * 100).alias("pct"),
    )
    .drop("bar_total")
)

pct_labels_mc = plot_df_pct.filter(pl.col("pct") >= 3).with_columns(
    pl.col("pct")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"{v:.0f}%", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("pct_label"),
)

(
    ggplot(plot_df_pct, aes(x="bar", y="pct", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.7)
    + geom_text(
        pct_labels_mc,
        aes(label="pct_label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=8,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(values=ALL_COLORS, breaks=ALL_COMPONENTS)
    + scale_y_continuous(expand=(0, 0, 0.02, 0))
    + facet_wrap("utility", ncol=4)
    + labs(x="", y="% of total", title="Revenue requirement vs. cost allocation (% of total)", fill="")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        figure_size=(16, 8),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=8),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, fontweight="bold"),
    )
)
```

```{python}
#| label: mc-residual-table

mc_table = (
    mc_residual
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("utility").cast(pl.Enum(utility_order)),
    )
    .sort("utility")
    .select(
        "utility",
        (pl.col("total_mc") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Marginal Cost ($M)"),
        (pl.col("total_residual") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Residual Cost ($M)"),
        (pl.col("total_bill") / 1e6).round(0).cast(pl.Int64).alias("Total Bill ($M)"),
        (pl.col("total_mc") / pl.col("total_bill") * 100).round(1).alias("MC % of Total"),
    )
)

mc_table
```
